diff --git a/src/click/core.py b/src/click/core.py
index cc65e89..554abc6 100644
--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -2355,6 +2355,12 @@ class Parameter:
     def process_value(self, ctx: Context, value: t.Any) -> t.Any:
         value = self.type_cast_value(ctx, value)
 
+        # A parameter is treated as required only if it was explicitly
+        # marked as such (self.required) and no value was actually
+        # supplied for it (value_is_missing checks for None, or an
+        # empty tuple when multiple values/nargs are expected). If both
+        # conditions hold, the CLI refuses to proceed and reports the
+        # parameter as missing.
         if self.required and self.value_is_missing(value):
             raise MissingParameter(ctx=ctx, param=self)

+++ b/tests/test_basic.py
@@ -236,9 +236,11 @@ def test_boolean_flag(runner, default, args, expect):
 
 @pytest.mark.parametrize(
     ("value", "expect"),

path = "tests/test_basic.py"
s = open(path).read()
old = '''    chain(
        ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
        ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),

        chain(
            ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
            ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
        )
    ),'''
assert old in s
s = s.replace(old, new)
open(path, "w").write(s)
EOF

--- a/src/click/core.py
+++ b/src/click/core.py
@@ -2982,6 +2982,11 @@ class Argument(Parameter):
         required: t.Optional[bool] = None,
         **attrs: t.Any,
     ) -> None:
+        # If the caller didn't explicitly say whether this argument is
+        # required, decide it automatically: an argument with a default
+        # value is optional (the default can stand in for user input),
+        # while one with no default is required unless nargs is 0 (which
+        # means it never consumes any values, so "required" is moot).
         if required is None:
             if attrs.get("default") is not None:
                 required = False

final_session_calls.command:
cd /work && python - <<'EOF'
import re
path = "tests/test_basic.py"
with open(path) as f:
    content = f.read()

old = '''    chain(
        ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
        ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
    ),'''
new = '''    list(
        chain(
            ((x, "True") for x in ("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")),
            ((x, "False") for x in ("0", "false", "f", "no", "n", "off")),
        )
    ),'''
assert old in content
content = content.replace(old, new)
with open(path, "w") as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF
